revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Izanagi-jingu]][[en:Izanagi Shrine]][[ja:伊弉諾神宮]][[zh:伊弉諾神宮]] {{Expand Japanese|伊弉諾神宮}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Izanagi Shrine | native_name = 伊弉諾神宮 | image = Izanagi-jingu Haiden.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Izanagi Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.460204|134.852498}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Izanagi|lt=Izanagi|ar|إزاناغي|az|İzanaqi|bcl|Izanagi|bn|ইজানাগি|bs|Izanagi|ca|Izanagi|cs|Izanagi a Izanami|da|Izanagi|el|Ιζανάγκι|en|Izanagi|eo|Izanagi|es|Izanagi|fa|ایزاناگی|fi|Izanaki|fr|Izanagi|he|איזאנגי|hr|Izanagi|hu|Izanagi|id|Izanagi|it|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|ka|იძანაგი|ko|이자나기|lt|Idzanagis|mk|Изанаги|mni|ꯏꯓꯥꯅꯥꯒꯤ|no|Izanagi|or|ଇଜାନାଗି|pt|Izanagi|ro|Izanagi|ru|Идзанаги|simple|Izanagi|sk|Izanagi|sl|Izanagi|sr|Изанаки|sv|Izanagi|ta|இசநாகி|th|อิซานางิ|tl|Izanagi|tr|İzanagi|uk|Ідзанаґі|vi|Izanagi|zh|伊邪那岐|qid=Q813858|1=Izanagi}} | website = {{Official website|https://kuniuminoshima.jp/%e4%bc%8a%e5%bc%89%e8%ab%be%e7%a5%9e%e5%ae%ae/}} }} {{nihongo|'''Izanagi Jingū'''|伊弉諾神宮}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awaji Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|List of Jingū|lt=jingū|en|List of Jingū|es|Santuario Jingū|fr|Sanctuaire jingū|ja|神宮|ko|신궁 (신토)|zh|神宮|qid=Q120680118|1=List of Jingū}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya|lt=ichinomiya|ca|Ichinomiya (Xinto)|de|Ichi-no-miya|en|Ichinomiya|fr|Ichi-no-miya|id|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|ko|이치노미야|pt|Ichinomiya (templo)|simple|Ichinomiya|th|อิจิโนมิยะ|uk|Ітіномія (титул)|zh|一宮|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} ** P1001: {{ill|Q1053717|ltq=Q1053717|qid=Q1053717|1=Awaji Province}} * {{ill|Q10898274|ltq=Q10898274|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} * {{ill|Myojin Taisha|lt=Myōjin Taisha|en|Myojin Taisha|fr|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|simple|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} * {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q845945|ltq=Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Awaji Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Awaji Province|ja|淡路国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11559808|1=List of Shikinaisha in Awaji Province}} ** Series ordinal: 1 ** P155: {{ill|Q135041283|ltq=Q135041283|qid=Q135041283|1=Ametachikarawono Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Ise-Kuruma Shrine|lt=Ise-Kuruma Shrine|qid=Q128866991|1=Ise-Kuruma Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q373107|ltq=Q373107|qid=Q373107|1=Awaji, Hyōgo}} * {{ill|Q1053717|ltq=Q1053717|qid=Q1053717|1=Awaji Province}} * {{ill|Tsuna District, Hyōgo|lt=Tsuna district|en|Tsuna District, Hyōgo|ja|津名郡|zh|津名郡|qid=Q7850141|1=Tsuna District, Hyōgo}} == coordinate location (P625) == * Izanami|da|Izanagi|el|Ιζανάγκι|en|Izanagi|eo|Izanagi|es|Izanagi|fa|ایزاناگی|fi|Izanaki|fr|Izanagi|he|איזאנגי|hr|Izanagi|hu|Izanagi|id|Izanagi|it|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|ka|იძანაგი|ko|이자나기|lt|Idzanagis|mk|Изанаги|mni|ꯏꯓꯥꯅꯥꯒꯤ|no|Izanagi|or|ଇଜାନାଗି|pt|Izanagi|ro|Izanagi|ru|Идзанаги|simple|Izanagi|sk|Izanagi|sl|Izanagi|sr|Изанаки|sv|Izanagi|ta|இசநாகி|th|อิซานางิ|tl|Izanagi|tr|İzanagi|uk|Ідзанаґі|vi|Izanagi|zh|伊邪那岐|qid=Q813858|1=Izanagi}} University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20160130164422/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/530001.html == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == Taisha|qid=Q134917279|1=Kanpei Taisha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == native label (P1705) == * {'text': '伊弉諾神宮', 'language': 'ja'} ** P1814: いざなぎじんぐう ** P2125: Izanagi-jingū == name in kana (P1814) == * いざなぎじんぐう == has part(s) of the class (P2670) == * {{ill|Haiden (Shinto)|lt=haiden|en|Haiden (Shinto)|es|Haiden (sintoísmo)|fa|هایدن (شینتو)|fi|Haiden|fr|Haiden|id|Haiden (Shinto)|it|Haiden|ja|拝殿|mk|Хаиден (Шинто)|pl|Haiden|pt|Haiden|ru|Хайдэн|tr|Haiden (Şinto)|qid=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} * {{ill|Q2044994|ltq=Q2044994|qid=Q2044994|1=Honden}} * {{ill|Q1069667|ltq=Q1069667|qid=Q1069667|1=Chōzuya}} == postal code (P281) == * 656-1521https://kuniuminoshima.jp == TripAdvisor ID (P3134) == * 2105917 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2140005019631 == Commons category (P373) == * Izanagi-jingu == Google Maps Customer ID (P3749) == * 4568735081946745150 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県淡路市多賀740', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '740 Taga, Awaji-shi, Hyōgo-ken 656-1521', 'language': 'en'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/011pzfks == Japan Search name ID (P6698) == * 伊弉諾神宮 == image of entrance (P9721) == * Izanagi-jingu, otorii-1.jpg {{commons category|Category:Izanagi-jingu}} [[EN:Izanagi Shrine]] [[JA:伊弉諾神宮]] [[ZH:伊弉諾神宮]] {{wikidata link|Q10884977}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name = Izanagi Shrine | Image = [[File:Izanagi-jingu, haiden-1.jpg|270px]]
House of worship | Location = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Awaji City|Q373107|qid=Q373107|1=Awaji, Hyōgo}}Taga 740 | Location = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|27|36|N|134|51|08|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Izanagi Shrine}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanami|1=Izanagi}}
{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=Meishindai|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
{{ill|lt=Awaji Province|Q1053717|qid=Q1053717|1=Awaji Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}
Old {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system #government shrine|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Kanpei Taisha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}
{{ill|lt=Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} | Founding = Age of the Gods (according to {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}/{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|qid=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}) | Main shrine = Sangensha {{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Chief priest = {{ill|lt=Takashi real name|Q115529376|qid=Q115529376|1=Takashi Honmyō}} | Aliases = Mr. Ichinomiya, Mr. Izanami | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 22nd|Q2526|qid=Q2526|1=April 22}}| Shinto ritual = Congee fortune-telling festival ({{ill|lt=January 15th|Q2260|qid=Q2260|1=January 15}} ({{ill|lt=Little New Year|Q137803901|qid=Q137803901|1=koshōgatsu}}))
Insect festival (July {{ill|lt=midsummer|Q11406993|qid=Q11406993|1=Crow-dipper sprouts}} day) | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=27|lat_sec=36|lon_deg=134|lon_min=51|lon_sec=08|label=Izanagi Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}}}} [[File:Izanagi-jingu, otorii-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Otorii}}]]'''Izanagi Jingu'' (Izanagi Jingu) is located in {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Awaji City|Q373107|qid=Q373107|1=Awaji, Hyōgo}} Taga. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinai Shrine|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), {{ill|lt=Awaji Province|Q1053717|qid=Q1053717|1=Awaji Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}. {{ill|lt=Modern Shrine Ranking System|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Old Shrine Ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|lt=Modern Shrine Ranking System|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Old Shrine Ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|lt=Modern Shrine Ranking System#Government Shrine|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Kanpei Taisha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and currently it is {{ill|lt=Beppyō Shrine|Q10898274|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Shrine Head Office|Q908077|qid=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}.The name of the former location, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Town (Tsuna District, Hyogo Prefecture)|Q5986243|qid=Q5986243|lt=Ichinomiya Town|1=Ichinomiya, Hyōgo (Tsuna)}} (currently Awaji City), originates from our company. Commonly known as ``Ikku-san'' and ``Izanou-san.'' On {{ill|lt=January 15th|Q2260|qid=Q2260|1=January 15}} ({{ill|lt=Little New Year|Q137803901|qid=Q137803901|1=koshōgatsu}}), the Awaji Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries Festival will be held in addition to the Ogayu Fortune Festival. {{ill|lt=2016|Q25245|qid=Q25245|1=2016}}{{ill|lt=April 25th|Q2531|qid=Q2531|1=April 25}} was certified as {{ill|lt=Japan Heritage|Q18382798|qid=Q18382798|1=Japan Heritage}} as one of the constituent cultural properties{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story030/ |title=STORY #030 “Awaji, the island of national birth” that decorates the beginning of “Kojiki” - The activities of the mariners who supported the ancient nation - |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-08-04 }}. === Enshrined God === * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} * {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami Mikoto|1=Izanami}} Both gods appear in the Japanese mythology {{ill|lt=Kokusanmi|Q3545163|qid=Q3545163|1=Kuniumi}} and {{ill|lt=Kamisanmi|Q3545159|qid=Q3545159|1=Kamiumi}}.Since the enshrined deity in "Yukyu Goki" is "Izanagi no Ichibushira," it is thought that originally only Izanagi was enshrined. . {{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|qid=Q18782|1=1931}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}6) "Ancient Shrine Document Copy" contains a petition regarding the enshrined deity, which states, The shrine is also of the highest rank, and the status of the shrine is Kanpei Taisha.The two deities, Izanami no Mikoto and Izanami no Mikoto, were born domestically and divinely, and these two deities should be enshrined regardless of the number of shrines listed in "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}." |Q3125096}} In {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|qid=Q7741|1=1870}}, {{ill|lt=Meito Prefecture|Q9610752|qid=Q9610752|1=Myōdō Prefecture}} designated Izanagi as the sole pillar. This is unfair as the subsidiary shrine of the Sesha was officially recognized. }} (1932) When we opened the main shrine, we found that Izami no Mikoto was also being enshrined as it had been handed down, so we requested permission to enshrine the two deities, Izanakomiko and Izami no Mikoto, with the request along with some materials, and {{ill|lt=1932|Q18743|qid=Q18743|1=1932}} (Showa 7) {{ill|lt=Minister of the Interior (Japan)|Q11267748|qid=Q11267748|lt=Minister of the Interior|1=Minister of Home Affairs}} ``We have received permission to increase the number of enshrined deities,'' and the shrine officially began to enshrine two deities.In addition, {{ill|lt=Ken Matsumae|Q11529297|qid=Q11529297|1=Takeshi Matsumae}} argues that the myth of the local god Izanagi was incorporated into the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Court|1=UNKNOWN}} mythology from Awaji Province.From Ken Matsumae, "Japanese Gods," Chuokoronsha Co., Ltd., September 1974. . According to Matsumae, it does not seem that the belief that Izana no Mikoto was the parent of the Imperial Ancestor God existed in the imperial court for a long time; the two gods were only incorporated after the mid-seventh century, and it was passed down by people from Awaji who were called to {{ill|lt=Daijōsai|Q11433653|qid=Q11433653|1=Daijosai}} rituals for Rabbit's Day, and by Awaji seamen who brought food to the imperial court. Furthermore, in ``{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jiroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', it is said that the reason why our company jumped from the 8th rank of Muppin to the highest rank of Ippin is that it was during this period that he was officially made the next relative of the Imperial Ancestor God. === History === === Founding === In ``{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' and ``{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|qid=Q813031|1=Kojiki}},'' it is said that Izanagi no Mikoto, who had finished domestic and divine births, rested in a mysterious palace (kakurinomiya, final abode) in Taga, the land where he first gave birth, {{ill|lt=Awaji Island|Q504373|qid=Q504373|1=Awaji Island}}, which is said to be the origin of our company. The other place that is said to be the ghost shrine of Izanagison is {{jalink|Taga Taisha|Q402091} in {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|qid=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}} }, which is written in the Shinpuku-ji book of Kojiki, ``The late Izanagi Ōkamija za'''tankai'''no'''Taga'''ya.'' (Izanagi no Okami is According to the description of ``Afumi no Tagani Masanari''. However, it is known that the deity enshrined at Taga Taisha Shrine was not Izanagison until the period of the Northern and Southern Courts, so it is not possible to connect the description in the Kojiki with Taga Taisha Shrine. In the Kojiki, ``Omi'' is usually referred to as ``Kindankai,'' and even in the same ``Kojiki,'' many manuscripts other than the Shinpukuji version refer to ``the late Iyanagi Daishinshaza''''Awaji''''. 'Taga'''ya.', and for various other reasons, there is a prevailing theory in academic circles that supports 'Awaji' rather than 'Awami' (Yukichi Takeda, Kojiro Naoki, etc.). Furthermore, in "Nihonshoki" it is consistently written as "Awaji", and there is no name that corresponds to "Omi". === Outline === In ancient literature, there is a mention of ``Izaku no Kami'' living on the island in the September article of the 5th year of ``{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' {{ill|lt=Emperor Richu|Q329704|qid=Q329704|1=Emperor Richū}}, and a description of ``Shimagami'' in the September 14th article of {{ill|lt=Emperor Konkyo|Q349258|qid=Q349258|1=Emperor Ingyō}}, and these are generally compared to our company. “{{ill|lt=Shinsho Kokufusho|Q11502312|qid=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}” {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|qid=Q1373191|lt=Daido|1=Daidō}} According to the 1st year ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|qid=Q30021|1=806}}), 13 {{ill|lt=Kamfudo|UNKNOWN|lt=Kanfu}} were allocated to "Tsunagami". Furthermore, in the official seal of September 13, {{ill|lt=Tenkei|Q1327340|qid=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=940|Q23830|qid=Q23830|1=940}}), it appears that 5 houses were added to ``Isana Shinmikojin.'' "{{ill|lt=Japan's Three Dynasty Chronicles|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}" {{ill|lt=Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} In the article dated January 27, 1985 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=Shinkai|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of ``Isanagi no Mikoto'' was promoted from the 8th class of the Muppin Order to the 8th class of the Ippin Order.{{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Extended|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} |Engishiki Shrine Name Book|Q11064932|Shin Name Book}}, {{ill|lt=Awaji Country|Q1053717|qid=Q1053717|1=Awaji Province}}{{ill|lt=Tsuna District|Q7850141|qid=Q7850141|1=Tsuna District, Hyōgo}} has "Awaji Isanaki Shrine It is listed as ``Meishin Taisha'' and is listed as {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}.{{ill|lt=Eiman|Q1193868|qid=Q1193868|1=Eiman}} June 1st year ({{ill|lt=1165|Q19676|qid=Q19676|1=1165}}) "Annual Tribute Order for Jingikan Shrines" Heian Ibun No. 3358 "Annual Tribute Order for Jingikan Shrines" edited by Rizo Takeuchi "Heian Ibun Ancient Documents Volume 7" Tokyodo Publishing Co., Ltd. Included in October 1963. 's description of "Awaji Province Ichinomiya ", {{ill|lt=Motohisa|Q1194276|qid=Q1194276|1=Genkyū}} April 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1205|Q5229|qid=Q5229|1=1205}}) "Awaji Kokushichosen" name="chousn">'''Chousen'' is an order given by {{ill|lt=Kokushi|Q1351234|qid=Q1351234|1=Kokushi (official)}}, who was not assigned to the local government, to officials in {{ill|lt=Kokushi|Q11421342|qid=Q11421342|1=kokuga}}. ``Awaji Kokushichosen'' is one of the ``Gokokuji Documents'' of Gokokuji Temple on Awaji Island, and is included in ``Hyogo Prefectural History Historical Materials Edition Medieval 1'', Hyogo Prefecture, November 1983, edited by the Hyogo Prefectural History Editorial Committee. Kamakura Ibun No. 3088 "Awaji Province Ota Bun" written in April 2012 ({{ill|lt=1223|Q5399|qid=Q5399|1=1223}}) "Awaji Province Ota Bun" written in April 2012 ({{ill|lt=1223|Q5399|qid=Q5399|1=1223}}) Included in "Kamakura Ibun Ancient Documents Volume 5" edited by Rizo Takeuchi, published by Tokyodo Publishing Co., Ltd., September 1973. 's description of ``One Shrine of Ichinomiya and One Shrine of the same Shrine'' indicates that it was considered to be Ichinomiya in Awaji Province from the end of {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}} to the beginning of {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}. For this reason, he was also called Ichinomiya Okami.In addition, the above document reveals the state of our company in {{ill|lt=Medieval #Japan|Q12554|qid=Q12554|lt=Medieval|1=Middle Ages}}. “Awaji Kokushicho Sen”From "Basic Research on the Ichinomiya System in Medieval Countries" edited by the Ichinomiya System Study Group in Medieval Countries, Iwata Shoin, February 2000. . Also, from the description in "Awaji Province Ota Bun" , we know that there was a {{ill|lt=Jingu-ji Temple|Q1074971|qid=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} in the early Kamakura period. According to the records of Myokyo-ji Temple, which was our {{ill|lt=Bettoji|Q11396755|qid=Q11396755|lt=Betto|1=Bettō-ji}}, Nakami Tamura, a descendant of {{ill|lt=Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979|qid=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}}, rebuilt the shrine in {{ill|lt=Koan|Q389180|qid=Q389180|1=Kōan (Kamakura period)}}3 ({{ill|lt=1280|Q5536|qid=Q5536|1=1280}}). {{ill|lt=Taikei|Q1193564|qid=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} 5th year ({{ill|lt=1525|Q6314|qid=Q6314|1=1525}}) Edited by Shinto Taikei Compilation Association, "Shinto Taikei Shrines Edition 41 Kii/Awaji Province" Included in Shinto Taikei Compilation Association, September 1987. According to , the Tamura clan was the Ichinomiya Shinto priest and feudal lord.{{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}}9 ({{ill|lt=1581|Q6717|qid=Q6717|1=1581}}) Tsuneharu Tamura was ordered by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|qid=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}} to take the lead in the battle against {{ill|lt=Katsuyori Takeda|Q662482|qid=Q662482|1=Takeda Katsuyori}}, but he did not obey and committed seppuku, resulting in the destruction of the Tamura clan.. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was revered by the {{ill|lt=Hachisuka clan|Q2975261|qid=Q2975261|lt=Hachisuka family|1=Hachisuka clan}}, the lord of {{ill|lt=Tokushima domain|Q3034557|qid=Q3034557|1=Tokushima Domain}}, and {{ill|lt=Motowa (Japan)|Q635245|qid=Q635245|lt=Genwa|1=Genna}} Obtained 10 {{ill|lt=stone (unit)|Q855048|qid=Q855048|lt=stone|1=Koku}} with the black seal letter in 7th year ({{ill|lt=1621|Q6812|qid=Q6812|1=1621}}). In addition, the Ishigami clan served as a hereditary Shinto priest, and the shrine's rituals were administered by both the shrine family and the temple family, but the two groups have been in conflict several times. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|qid=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} ({{ill|lt=1661|Q7005|qid=Q7005|1=1661}}), our company's ``Two Pillars of Engi'' was created. Since the Engi was read in front of the feudal lord and the envoys when they visited the shrine, a dispute arose over whether the shrine family or the temple family would serve. ``Gunke Monogatari'' includes a description of ``the only turmoil in Ichinomiya'' that lasted for six years during the Horeki era.{{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|qid=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1752|Q7618|qid=Q7618|1=1752}})'s ``Gunke Monogatari'' reveals the existence of six shrine families who did not have a danke and held festivals by distributing the territory of Ichinomiya, and six temple priests at the end of Myokyo-ji Temple, which was a betto, and these six shrine families are thought to be Jingu temples. However, according to ``A copy of the response letter with an addendum from Miyabo's husband'', five of the six shrine priests, Tozobo, Torinbo, Engyobo, Shinbo, and Jizobo, withdrew, leaving only Miyabo. {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|qid=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 8 ({{ill|lt=1825|Q6997|qid=Q6997|1=1825}}) From the description in "Awajikusa Volume 4", it is said that the Jinguji temple, which was also called {{ill|lt=Hosso sect|Q349849|qid=Q349849|1=East Asian Yogācāra}} and {{ill|lt=Shingon sect|Q550182|qid=Q550182|1=Shingon Buddhism}}, was founded by Koji. (Japan)|Q1143910|Koji}} was converted to {{ill|lt=Nichiren sect|Q206611|qid=Q206611|1=Nichiren-shū}} by Mr. Tamura in the first year ({{ill|lt=1555|Q6579|qid=Q6579|1=1555}}), and {{jali nk|Genroku|Q1325001}} From the description in ``A copy of the reply letter with an addendum from the priest of the temple priest'' dated August 12 ({{ill|lt=1699|Q7710|qid=Q7710|1=1699}}), it is known that shrine priests who did not comply with the conversion were expelled. For this reason, there is a theory that the Tamura clan had jurisdiction over Ichinomiya at the end of the medieval period.In {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|qid=Q7741|1=1870}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}3), {{ill|lt=Meito Prefecture|Q9610752|qid=Q9610752|1=Myōdō Prefecture}} decided to enshrine one deity, Izanagison, instead of two. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|qid=Q7746|1=1871}} (Meiji 4), it was promoted to Kokuhei-chusha, and in {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|qid=Q7821|1=1885}} (Meiji 18), it was promoted to {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system #government shrine|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Kanpei Taisha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=1932|Q18743|qid=Q18743|1=1932}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}7), it was approved to enshrine Izanari as the enshrined deity, as described above. In {{ill|lt=1948|Q5165|qid=Q5165|1=1948}} (Showa 23), it was added to {{ill|lt=Beppyō Shrine|Q10898274|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Shinja Honcho|Q908077|qid=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|qid=Q18581|1=1954}} (Showa 29), the name was changed from Izano Shrine to the current Izano Shrine. {{ill|lt=1995|Q2068|qid=Q2068|1=1995}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}}7) It suffered significant damage, including the collapse of the first torii gate in the {{ill|lt=Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|Q11392882|qid=Q11392882|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake}} of {{ill|lt=January 17|Q2264|qid=Q2264|1=January 17}}. The torii gate was rebuilt in November of the same year, with the format changed to {{ill|lt=Torii #form|Q234731|qid=Q234731|lt=Shinmei Torii|1=Torii}}, with the dedication of {{ill|lt=parishioners|Q125562175|qid=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}}. There is a {{ill|lt=fragrant wood|Q87216239|qid=Q87216239|1=fragrant wood}} stone monument in the precincts. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|qid=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} January 27, 3rd year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}), from Mupin-kun 8th class to Ippin-kun 8th class ('{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jiroki|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') - Written as 'Isanagi no Mikoto'. === Precincts === * Main shrine - In {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|qid=Q7783|1=1879}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12 years), it was rebuilt about 10 meters ahead of its current location, but in {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|qid=Q7808|1=1881}} (Meiji 14 years), a mausoleum, which was a forbidden area behind the main shrine, was created and relocated on top of it. It is a Sangen shrine {{ill|lt=Nagazukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} and is connected to the shrine hall by a roof. * The Hall - Built in 1881 (Meiji 14). * Transept * Chumon - Built in 1881 (Meiji 14). * Corridor * Purification Hall/Shinsenden * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|qid=Q7812|1=1882}} (Meiji 15). It has a copper plate roof and doubles as a dance hall. * Mikoshi Storehouse - Built in {{ill|lt=Bunka (era name)|Q1373146|qid=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}}5 ({{ill|lt=1808|Q6914|qid=Q6914|1=1808}}) by {{ill|lt=Tokushima Domain|Q3034557|qid=Q3034557|1=Tokushima Domain}} lord {{ill|lt=Haruaki Hachisuka|Q3125457|qid=Q3125457|1=Hachisuka Haruaki}}. * {{ill|lt=Emperor Showa|Q34479|qid=Q34479|1=Hirohito}}'s own {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|qid=Q158722|lt=campus|1=Camphora officinarum}} - It was planted on November 30th, {{ill|lt=1922|Q2165|qid=Q2165|1=1922}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}11) by Emperor Showa, who was still {{ill|lt=crown prince|Q207293|qid=Q207293|1=Crown prince}}. * {{ill|lt=Ise Shrine|Q687168|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Kodai Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} Distant Worship Hall - Ise Shrine is at the same latitude as Jingu Shrine, and is located due east from here.* Higashijinmon - Built during the Edo period by the Hachisuka clan, the lord of the Tokushima domain. It was renovated in the first year of {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|qid=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} ({{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|qid=Q7671|1=1688}}). The oldest building in the shrine. * {{ill|lt=Kiichiro Higuchi|Q974210|qid=Q974210|1=Kiichiro Higuchi}}{{ill|lt=Lieutenant General|Q152951|qid=Q152951|1=Lieutenant general}} Statue * Memento Hall * Nishijinmon - Built during the Edo period by the Hachisuka clan, the lord of the Tokushima domain. It was renovated in the first year of Genroku (1688). The oldest building in the shrine. * Shrine office * VIP hall * Sanshuden * East Award Office Building - Built in {{ill|lt=1998|Q2089|qid=Q2089|1=1998}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}}10). *West Award Office Building - Built in 1998 (Heisei 10). * Main gate (Omote Shinmon) - Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1883|Q7816|qid=Q7816|1=1883}} (Meiji 16). It used to be Zuishinmon. *Tea room “Asuan” * Hojo-no-Kamiike Pond - This is said to be the remains of a moat that was said to have been centered around the imperial mausoleum at the Yugu ruins. A {{ill|lt=carp|Q81110|qid=Q81110|lt=carp|1=Common carp}} is released to pray for life, and a {{ill|lt=turtle|Q223044|qid=Q223044|lt=turtle|1=Turtle}} is released to pray for recovery. * Sun Road Signpost - A monument that shows the relationship between the movement of the {{ill|lt=sun|Q525|qid=Q525|1=Sun}} and famous shrines centered around Izanagi Shrine. Izanagi-jingu, honden.jpg|Main shrine Izanagi-jingu, haraeden.jpg|Haraeden Izanagi-jingu, Main Gate 001.jpg|OmotejinmonIzanagi-jingu, Sacred Pond 001.jpg|Sacred Pond Izanagi-jingu, torii.jpg|Torii in the precincts === Setsusha === * Left and right shrines - Deities enshrined: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|qid=Q595520|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomison|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} * Kashima Shrine/Sumiyoshi Shrine - Enshrined as the god of protection of agriculture and good luck and long lifeAccording to the local information board. . * Settsu Shrine/Kama Shrine - Guardian deity of sake brewing and brewing. is worshiped as the god of warding off disaster and fire protection. * Iwakusu Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Hiruko|Q1067028|qid=Q1067028|lt=Ebisu no Mikoto|1=Hiruko}}. It is enshrined at the base of a large camphor tree (designated by Hyogo Prefecture as a natural monument) that is approximately 900 years old. It is said to be the god of childbirth and safe childbirth. It has been enshrined since the middle of the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. * Awaji Soreisha Shrine - Enshrined deity: Over 8,000 spirits of enlightened people, wise men, and people of merit from {{ill|lt=Awaji Island|Q504373|qid=Q504373|1=Awaji Island}}. {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|qid=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9) Built. * Yeonju Palace * Outside company ** Hama Shrine (Hamanomiya) - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanamison|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanamison|1=Izanami}}. Sessha. *** Otoshi Ninja Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Otoshi Ninja God|Q10933447|qid=Q10933447|1=Oogotooshionokami}} *** {{ill|lt=Genital Worship|Q11072719|qid=Q11072719|lt=Yin Yang Stone|1=sex worship}} Izanagi-jingu, Auxiliary Kamado-jinja and Neno-jinja 001.jpg|Kamado Shrine/Neno Shrine Izanagi-jingu, Sumiyoshi-jinja and Kashima-jinja.jpg|Sumiyoshi Shrine/Kashima Shrine Izanagi-jingu, Sau-jinja.jpg|Left and right shrines Izanagi-jingu, Iwakusu-jinja.jpg|Iwakusu Shrine Izanagi-jingu, Auxiliary Soreisha Shrine 001.jpg|Awaji Soreisha Shrine Izanagi-jingu, Hama-jinja, shaden.jpg|Hama Shrine (Hamanomiya) === Cultural assets === === Hyogo Prefecture designated natural monument === [[File:Izanagi-jingu, kusu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|couple couple}}]] * Couple Kusu at Izano Shrine - Designated on March 9, 1973 {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf |title=List of prefecture-designated cultural properties |publisher=Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education Cultural Properties Division |accessdate=2016-01-22}}. === Awaji City designated tangible cultural property === * 9 divine statues === Local information === ; Location :* {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Awaji City|Q373107|qid=Q373107|1=Awaji, Hyōgo}}Taga 740 ; Transportation access :* Bus: {{ill|lt=Awaji Kotsu|Q11559804|qid=Q11559804|1=Awaji Kōtsū}}, {{ill|lt=Shinki Bus|Q11589421|qid=Q11589421|1=Shinki Bus}} (Sannomiya/Nishiura Line {{ill|lt=Highway Bus|Q3182239|qid=Q3182239|1=highway bus}}) and {{ill|lt=Awaji City Life Sightseeing Bus Route|Q17192440|qid=Q17192440|1=Awajin Awahime Bus}} (Awajin Awahime Bus) 1 (clockwise)/2 (counterclockwise)/11 (southern sightseeing tour) {{ill|lt=system number (Bus)|Q11604910|qid=Q11604910|lt=Route|1=Route}} Get off at "Izano Jingu-mae" bus stop (short walk after getting off) ; around :* {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefectural Awaji Kaori no Park|Q11393062|qid=Q11393062|1=Hyogo Prefectural Awaji Kaori no Park}} - Adjacent. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor={{ill|lt=Rizo
